title: Dad Wisdom
html:
Dad Wisdom

A recurring class of notes: short aphorisms, parables, or warnings that Yahir captures from his father, usually with a "-dad" attribution at the end of the line. The pattern is consistent enough across years to function as its own genre in the corpus — a man who isn't a writer, transmitting knowledge through compressed parables to a son who is. The dad in the corpus is unnamed, never quoted at length, and almost always shows up either as a wisdom-source or as a wound — sometimes in the same week.

The cleanest specimen is 2021 04 19 circus parable — a single paragraph dictated as told by his dad on April 18, 2021, about an 18-year-old who walks up to a circus looking for work. The manager scoffs ("can you sweep? mop?"), then asks what the kid actually does. "I can imitate birds." "I have tigers and elephants." Mid-sentence, the kid gets up and starts flying away. Yahir captured this at 18 himself, while applying to retail jobs (2020 03 30 facebook pandemic watchlist). The latent potential parable reads as a self-message his dad fed him at exactly the right moment, and as the cleanest articulation of the fear of not doing inversion: undersold capacity, no one asks the right question.

Other confirmed dad-attributed lines: "Impulse decision will kill you — dad" (in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream, surrounded by jazz, Web3 ideas, and ChatGPT product wedges). The reading log on One Hundred Years of Solitude (2022 03 11 one hundred years of solitude reading log) doesn't have an explicit "-dad" tag, but the lineage-as-fate themes Yahir keeps pulling out — "his lineage is like a wheel of rearing events that would continue on for eternity if it weren't for the determination of the axel" — read as filtered through the same paternal-inheritance lens. The Christmas Eve 2022 episode (2022 12 25 christmas eve 2022) is the inverse: dad blackout drunk, Valeria slapping him, Yahir spitting at him — and yet, in the same note, "He is caring about us way behind most dads, and obviously holds down a house."

The 2025 fragment in 2025 03 15 than usual recently — "My dad is getting better but I know he's gonna relax just because nothing has changed" — confirms the pattern continues: Yahir refusing to either canonize or abandon his father, holding the parable-source and the wound in the same hand. The dad-wisdom genre exists because the dad is hard. The aphorisms are the thing the family can keep.

Source summaries

  • 2021 04 19 circus parable
  • 2022 03 11 one hundred years of solitude reading log
  • 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream
  • 2022 12 25 christmas eve 2022
  • first batch 2026 04 26
